subject: feat(modules): add contract upload and conversion to modules
body:
Upload traditional contracts (.txt, .docx, .pdf) and convert them into
Sancio's module-based structure. Client-side extraction with no backend
changes needed.

Key design decisions:
- PDF library (pdfjs-dist) lazy-loaded via dynamic import with Vite-bundled
  worker — zero cost for non-PDF users, works offline (no CDN dependency)
- Heuristic section splitting at depth 0 only by default to avoid module
  proliferation; sub-section splitting is opt-in
- Batch creation in parallel groups of 4 via Promise.allSettled to handle
  SA mobile latency on 30+ module uploads
- Source tracking via [Source: filename] note convention — syncs to both
  parties via Supabase, searchable, no schema changes needed
- Duplicate detection via text hash in localStorage
- Encoding detection: BOM → UTF-8 strict → Windows-1252 fallback
- Oversized sections (>10k chars) split via paragraph → sentence →
  word-boundary fallback chain

New files:
- src/utils/documentParser.ts — text extraction for .txt/.docx/.pdf
- src/utils/contractSplitter.ts — heuristic section splitting
- src/components/modules/ContractUploadPreview.tsx — preview/edit UI

Modified files:
- src/components/modules/TemplateSelector.tsx — tabbed UI with upload tab
- src/pages/AgreementDetailPage.tsx — batch creation handler

Tags: feature, modules, upload, parsing, pdf, docx
